title: Sequences & Series of Functions
description: Module 3 — Pointwise vs uniform convergence, power & Taylor series

edges.label: fix \(x\) first, THEN take \(\lim_{n\to\infty} f_n(x)\) — this is \_\_\_ convergence
edges.hint: Convergence at each individual point separately.

edges.label: one \(N\) works for ALL \(x\) — stronger than pointwise; called \_\_\_
the function \(f(x) := \lim_n f_n(x)\) is the \_\_\_ of \(\{f_n\}\)
edges.hint: It is the pointwise ___ of the sequence.

edges.label: uniform limit of continuous functions: is the limit \(f\) automatically continuous?
under uniform conv, \(\int (\lim f_n) = \lim \int f_n\) — the two operations \_\_\_
edges.hint: You can swap their order — they ___.

edges.label: preserving derivatives needs uniform conv of \(\{f_n'\}\) too — NOT just of \(\{f_n\}\); answer Y/N: works?
edges.hint: Uniform conv of \(f_n\) ALONE is not enough — ___.

edges.label: form partial sums \(S_N(x) = \sum_{n=1}^N f_n(x)\); their limit is a \_\_\_ of functions
edges.hint: \(\sum f_n(x)\) is the analog of \(\sum a_n\) but each term is now a function.

edges.label: if \(|f_n(x)| \le M_n\) and \(\sum M_n < \infty\), \(\sum f_n\) converges \_\_\_
edges.hint: The M-test gives the STRONGEST type of convergence — ___.

edges.label: special form \(\sum a_n x^n\) — each term is \(x\) raised to a \_\_\_
a power series converges for \(|x| < R\); this \(R\) is the \_\_\_ of convergence
edges.hint: Geometrically, \(R\) measures the half-width of the convergence region — its ___.

edges.label: the set of all \(x\) where \(\sum a_n x^n\) converges is an \_\_\_ centered at \(0\)
edges.hint: A connected piece of \(\mathbb{R}\), possibly with endpoints — an ___.

edges.label: with coefficients \(a_n = f^{(n)}(c)/n!\), the power series equals \(f\) on a \_\_\_ around \(c\)
edges.hint: Local agreement between \(f\) and its expansion holds in some open ___ of \(c\).

edges.label: a function that equals its Taylor series in a nbhd of every point of its domain is called \_\_\_
edges.hint: Greek root meaning "to break apart" — such a function is ___.
